programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in South America, where animals must specialise if they are to carve out a niche. In Patagonia, a puma mother draws on a lifetime's experience to catch prey three times her weight, while rarely seen Andean bears clamber 30 metres into the cloud forest canopy to find elusive fruit. Poison dart frogs use ingenious methods to keep their tadpoles safe, anacondas stalk capuchin monkeys, and swifts make death-defying flights through Igauzu Falls on the border between Argentina and Brazil(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team heads to the Balcaskie Estate in the East Neuk of Fife, where Charlotte Smith tries her hand at competitive ploughing, Ellie Harrison meets some local food producers, and Steve Brown is at Pittenweem harbour as the fishing boats bring in their catches. Meanwhile, it's a tense time for Adam Henson down on the farm as his cattle are tested for TB(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs and Isolated for millions of years, leaving weird and wonderful animals found nowhere else on Earth. In its jungles, a cassowary - one of the most dangerous birds in the world - stands six feet tall, while kangaroos and wombats brave snowstorms, and gum-tree forests are filled with predators. In its red desert heart, reptiles drink through their skin and huge flocks of wild budgerigars swirl in search of water. On secret islands Tasmanian devils roam, and offshore thousands of sharks gather for a rare event.(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman host from Blackpool's Tower Ballroom as they announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores taking part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. With music by Westlife(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: After escaping from the clutches of the Gobblers, Lyra finds help from an unlikely source. With their assistance she is able to piece together more about her past and keep herself safe from Mrs Coulter and the Magisterium. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen and Ruth Wilson(n)

programme.title: The War of the Worlds
programme.desc: When a mysterious capsule lands in Woking, Surrey, in 1905 lovers George and Amy join the curious crowd to examine it. As events unfold, the couple are caught in the middle of a Martian invasion of Earth, fighting for their love and their lives against an enemy beyond their comprehension. Adaptation of HG Wells' sci-fi classic, starring Rafe Spall, Eleanor Tomlinson, Rupert Graves and Robert Carlyle(n)

programme.title: The Richard Dimbleby Lecture
programme.desc: Jonathan Dimbleby introduces this year's lecture from London's Design Museum, with engineer and computer scientist Tim Berners-Lee talking about his aspirations for the future of the world wide web, which he invented in 1989(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield and Tottenham Hotspur v Arsenal at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium(n)

programme.title: Gary Lineker: My Grandad's War
programme.desc: The Match of the Day presenter retraces the footsteps of his grandfather Stanley Abbs to explore a brutal but often overlooked chapter of the Second World War. Using the official war diary kept by Stanley's unit as a guide, Gary revisits the places where he was stationed and meets experts who help him piece together what happened to his grandfather during the Italian campaign. Along the way, Gary meets veterans who share first-hand testimonies to and help him understand the reality of combat in Italy(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood spends 24 hours working as a firefighter in Texas, but just getting the gear on in time is a tough job for him, let alone confronting his fear of heights(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell views a Macclesfield house with worrying cracks, Dion Dublin finds a semi in Cumbria that offers potential and Martin Roberts is puzzled by an unusual lot in Kent(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A man uses a burglary as an opportunity to exaggerate his claim, while friendships are tested as hard-up students resort to fraudulent measures. Naga Munchetty presents(n)

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: City of London police go on the trail of an international fraudster, while one of Britain's largest-ever criminal enterprises is taken down by Border Force and the Royal Navy(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items at the Royal Cornwall Showground, while presenter Charlie Ross visits Smeaton's Tower in Plymouth(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel and Zara conduct interviews for a new practice manager, while Karen finds herself more deeply involved in Martha's personal life and Al encounters an odd family set-up(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth struggles to protect those closest to him when an old foe is unexpectedly released from prison(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a house-hunting mission in the Northumberland countryside with a couple who have their heart set on a character stone property with holiday let potential(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne visits Woking Recycling Centre, on a mission to save three tip-bound items. He enlists the help of Sarah Peterson to give an outdated telephone cabinet a radical revamp(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which experts try to restore family heirlooms and treasured possessions to their former glories. In the first edition, Jay Blades and the team take on an ornate walnut piano stool, a speedometer from a 1920s vintage car and baby's wicker crib(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Everyone is reeling from the events of last week but the big question is - has Sharon managed to keep her secret hidden? Ben realises Callum is seriously considering joining the police and admits that would be the end of their relationship. Mick encourages Linda to drum up business in his bid to win a best pub competition(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites comedian and actor Rob Delaney, presenter Davina McCall and actor and musician Laurence Fox to express their anger over individuals, events and objects that upset them, hoping to see them consigned to oblivion. Their gripes include people who order starters, 12-string guitars and cyclists wearing Lycra(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Government announced the closure of investigations into alleged war crimes in Iraq and Afghanistan before a single soldier was prosecuted. But has there been a cover-up at the highest levels of the British military? Reporter Richard Bilton meets UK detectives who talk for the first time about how they were prevented from prosecuting soldiers suspected of serious crimes, revealing evidence that suggests the Ministry of Defence and senior officers were involved in the cover-up of torture and illegal killings(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor is jealous when new pupil Gavin - who has childhood ties with Jock - arrives at school, and he is determined not to be ousted as best friend. Conor struggles to stand up to Gavin - but it's not easy as the newcomer's mother has just started teaching at the school. Meanwhile, a spate of thefts leads Principal Walsh to patrolling the school grounds to monitor any potential stealing(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space tell stories of life on our planet from a brand new perspective, to reveal new discoveries and explore Earth's most remote habitats. In the third episode, cameras unveil stories behind some of the planet's most intriguing colours, including the lights of the aurora, which tower in vertical streaks hundreds of kilometres high. Elsewhere, cameras also capture vast phytoplankton blooms in the ocean, and millions of rapeseed flowers in bloom in China's Yunnan province(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia encounters a passive-aggressive lift(n)

programme.title: Live International Rugby League
programme.desc: Papua New Guinea v Great Britain (Kick-off 7.30am). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the fourth and final Test for the Lions, which takes place at Oil Search National Football Stadium in Port Moresby. With commentary by Dave Woods and Brian Noble(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Steve Backshall continues his quest to encounter the world's 60 deadliest animals in their natural habitats. Heading to South Africa, he goes in search of the great white shark and has a trick up his sleeve to demonstrate its hunting prowess. The crew also observes cheetahs, a trio of the nation's most venomous snakes and the powerful secretary bird(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present the final, featuring the five heat winners and one of the runners-up. Judges Sethu Vijayakumar, Noel Sharkey and Dr Lucy Rodgers decide the winner, while Jonathan Pearce provides commentary on the mayhem(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Dr Ranj Singh and Dr Zoe Williams go looking for antiques in Lancashire and Greater Manchester, aided by experts Stephanie Connell and Tim Medhurst. Ranj visits a Blackpool dance hall and Zoe joins the crew of a steam train before attending an auction in Harrogate, where they hope to make a profit on their purchases(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the latest heat, Monica Galetti challenges three of the chefs to employ deft knife skills to spatchcock a quail before cooking it with rocket pesto in just 15 minutes. The remaining contestants prepare Marcus Wareing's beignets with chocolate sauce, before all six get the chance to redeem any earlier mistakes when they cook their own signature dish for Monica, Marcus and Gregg Wallace. The judges then decide which three have done enough to make it through to the quarter-final stage of the competition(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first three chefs recreate Monica's razor clams with a shallot, caper and tomato sauce, before the second group of three take on Marcus' calves liver with a Bordelaise sauce. In the next round, the six chefs aim to impress the judges with a dish of their own design, with just one hour and 15 minutes to prepare it(n)

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage from day seven at the O2 in London, featuring the opening semi-final of the singles competition. Twelve months ago, record six-time winner Roger Federer suffered a shock straight-sets defeat to Germany's Alexander Zverev, while Serbian Novak Djokovic overcame South Africa's Kevin Anderson to reach the final. Analysis and commentary comes from Tim Henman, Andrew Castle and Boris Becker(n)

programme.title: International Rugby League
programme.desc: Papua New Guinea v Great Britain. Mark Chapman presents highlights of the fourth and final Test for the Lions, which took place at Oil Search National Football Stadium in Port Moresby. With commentary by Dave Woods and Brian Noble(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: After three months at sea, the test pilots from the Royal Navy and RAF have successfully landed the F35 jets on the flight deck of HMS Queen Elizabeth for the first time, and even tested launching and landing in hurricane conditions. But with just a few weeks to go before the warship arrives in New York in spectacular style, they are about to try a method of landing never attempted before, which carries great risks but could revolutionise carrier operations globally. Narrated by Caroline Catz(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Mainwaring lands himself in trouble with Warden Hodges, accused of leaving a light burning in the church hall during a blackout, and receives a summons to appear in court. Comedy first aired in 1972, starring Arthur Lowe and Bill Pertwee(n)

programme.title: Wales: Land of the Wild
programme.desc: Documentary following Welsh wildlife. The first day of spring has a sting in its tail as snow still covers the land. These are challenging times for the Carneddau pony, an ancient breed that lives on the tops of Snowdonia's highest peaks. As the sun slowly strengthens, adders head off on the hunt, dippers begin nest building and black grouse fight for the right to mate(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the south east of Australia from Canberra, the seat of government, to the multi-ethnic heart of its second largest city, Melbourne. Along the way he discovers the history of the Ashes at Melbourne cricket ground, learns about the exploits of outlaw Ned Kelly and visits the home of superstar soprano Dame Nellie Melba. He also rides on the Puffing Billy narrow gauge steam railway, built in 1900, and has a go at shearing a sheep(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig looks at 'quacks' and 'quackery', with regular panellist Alan Davies and guest players Victoria Coren Mitchell, Stephen K Amos and Claudia Winkleman. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Novels That Shaped Our World
programme.desc: The second documentary examining fictional writing from three different perspectives considers the subject of race and colonialism. It looks at books including Robinson Crusoe, the first novel ever published in English, in 1719, whose hero was a slave trader, and two famous American novels published more than a century apart - Harriet Beecher Stowe's Uncle Tom's Cabin and Toni Morrison's Beloved. The programme also comes up to date with titles such as Malorie Blackman's Noughts and Crosses and Paul Beatty's The Sell Out(n)

programme.title: Pose
programme.desc: The House of Evangelista are set to kill two birds with one stone, as they plan a press stunt aimed at raising Aids awareness and antagonising Frederica at the same time. Meanwhile, Angel is introduced to the less savoury sides of modelling. As her big break approaches, will she be able to stay on track?(n)

programme.title: Pose
programme.desc: There's turmoil in the House of Evangelista as Damon - feeling betrayed by Ricky - turns on his housemates and reveals everyone else's secrets. Blanca tries to manage the crisis as best she can, but even she may not be as impartial as she thinks she is(n)

programme.title: Jimi: All Is By My Side (2013)
programme.desc: Biopic of Jimi Hendrix following the events of 1966, when he left New York to spend a year living in London. The relationships he developed over that time would shape his career as a musician, culminating in his famous appearance at the Monterey Pop Festival. Starring Andre Benjamin, Hayley Atwell and Imogen Poots(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: The abbot welcomes the delegates to the conference with a lavish supper. Bernard Gui asks to be introduced to William's novice Adso and takes a particular interest when told he is German, believing him to be a spy sent by the emperor. Salvatore warns Remigio that they should flee since he is certain that Gui has recognised him. In the woods outside the abbey, Anna is found wounded and unconscious in the woods by the Occitan girl, who tends to her injuries, and is later visited by Adso. John Turturro, Rupert Everett and Greta Scarano star(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at themes beginning with the letter C, as part of her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters(n)

programme.title: The Great Chelsea Garden Challenge
programme.desc: The three remaining competitors visit the RHS Headquarters in Wisley for the grand final of the garden design competition. Here, they are given five days - and their biggest budget yet - to put together showpiece gardens that will impress judges James Alexander-Sinclair and Ann-Marie Powell, and prove they are worthy of going to Chelsea. Joe Swift provides expert tips, with the winning designer earning the chance to put together a garden for the Main Avenue at RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2015(n)

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don travels to South America, home to more than 50,000 endemic plant species. Beginning in Brazil, the intrepid horticulturist views the private oasis of artist Burle Marx, and takes a trip by boat to the unique floating gardens of the Amazon, before visiting the coast of Chile - where an agriculturalist has created a landscape in tune with its craggy locale(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker is with the Essex Wildlife Trust on Fingringhoe Wick - a newly created stretch of saltmarsh - as he helps the team do its first-ever winter fish survey, Margherita Taylor hears about a project to preserve the spirit of the Dig for Victory campaign, before joining Adrian Thomas from the RSPB to learn about the organisation's new sound archive. Adam Henson meets a farmer feeding his cows charcoal, and Tom Heap asks if enough is being done in rural areas to support veterans(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The six strongest chefs from the heats compete in the quarter-final, inventing a dish with onion as the key ingredient. After the weakest two are sent home, the others cook two courses for three critics - William Sitwell, Tracey MacLeod and Amol Rajan. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef continues his 'journey without maps', leaving the main routes and tourist hotspots behind to embark on a personal road trip along picturesque and remote backroads. Here, he heads through France's eastern border regions of Alsace and Jura - famous for Comte cheese and home to his favourite French dish of choucroute garni. He then begins to head inland, visiting Burgundy for a memorable glass of Auxey-Duresses wine, and sampling oeuf en meurette with a difference. Following a memorable Sunday lunch near the French/Swiss border, he is inspired to recreate a traditional blueberry tart(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actor Neil Morrissey and his friend the TV presenter JJ Chalmers search for bargains in Cheshire and Staffordshire, with the help of Izzie Balmer and Charles Hanson. Along the way, Neil has a go at some elaborate ceramic decoration, while JJ gets dressed up as a cow and joins a troupe of dancing villagers(n)

programme.title: From Here to Eternity (1953)
programme.desc: A former boxer stationed at an American army base in peacetime Hawaii is manipulated by a sadistic superior, and a sergeant has an affair with a married officer's wife. However, their lives are set to change dramatically as the Japanese launch an attack on Pearl Harbour. Second World War drama, with Burt Lancaster, Deborah Kerr, Montgomery Clift, Frank Sinatra, Ernest Borgnine and Donna Reed(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The show comes from the Old Royal Naval College in Greenwich, where finds include a collection of fans and a signed postcard. Paul Martin takes a tour of the Cutty Sark(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Architect Piers Taylor and actress Caroline Quentin explore unusual homes built in or near areas of forest. After trips to properties near Madrid and the Catskill Mountains in New York State, they arrive in Piha, New Zealand, to a house built within an indigenous forest of pohutukawa trees. Navigating very strict environmental laws, this wooden-cladded and glass-roofed property mimics the branches of the surrounding trees, while its huge sliding glass walls open up to allow the surrounding forest to become an intrinsic part of the house itself(n)

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the final on day eight at the O2 in London, where Alexander Zverev was victorious last year. The German gained revenge for a group-stage defeat at the hands of Novak Djokovic, producing an excellent performance to overcome the Serbian great 6-4, 6-3 and claim the most prestigious title of his career so far, With analysis and commentary from Tim Henman, Andrew Castle and Boris Becker(n)

programme.title: Reggie in China
programme.desc: Reggie Yates travels to China and visits four very different cities. His first stop is Shenzhen, which over the past 40 years has grown from a region dominated by fishing and farmland to a bustling metropolis that is home to 12 million people and at the centre of the country's technological revolution. He meets the inventors of an earpiece that uses algorithms to translate speech into other languages, discovers more about the differing attitudes between generations, and witnesses a demonstration of a flying motorcycle(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian takes a tour of countries that are rarely visited by tourists to get a sense of the local culture and find out if they deserve their reputation. His first destination is Haiti, a country mostly famous for a dictatorship and a series of natural disasters. Accompanied by journalist Jeremy Dupin, he visits the iron market, attends vodou ceremonies, and explores a cathedral destroyed by an earthquake(n)

programme.title: The Hurt Locker (2008)
programme.desc: Two American bomb disposal experts operating in Iraq are assigned a new leader after their previous one dies in action. However, the sergeant's reckless attitude and love of battle consistently puts them at risk. Kathryn Bigelow's Oscar-winning drama, starring Jeremy Renner, Anthony Mackie, Guy Pearce and Ralph Fiennes(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Brighton, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The owners of a traditional Edwardian end-of-terrace, a Victorian terrace brought crashing into the 21st century, and a terrace that has been stretched to its limit all compete(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: A paranoid Jac feels she is the centre of hospital gossip when word spreads Emma is now living with Jonny. Elliot's concern for her grows, but feels unable to tell her his secret. Lofty resolves to put his new family first, but even the basics of parenting stir up trouble between him, Helen and Dom. Sacha struggles with his feelings for Essie, which seem doomed to go unrequited. Guest starring Vicky Entwistle (Coronation Street)(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood lines up with some serious firepower, working as a US marshal in San Antonio, Texas. His mentor is Deputy Chris Bozeman, who's spent the last 15 years arresting dangerous and wanted men. After rigorous firearms and tactical training, Dom is briefed on new intel and joins live cases, executing arrest warrants on armed suspects around the city(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Hugh Bonneville narrates a documentary looking at the habits of the family of animals and the skills they have developed to survive a range of environments around the world. The film explores how bears across the world have overcome the challenges of life - from finding food and raising the cubs to confronting rivals and habitat loss - all thanks to brains, brawn and a remarkable ability to adapt. Part of the Natural World strand(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day.(n)

programme.title: Street Auction
programme.desc: Paul Martin helps raise funds for a deserving member of a local community, using hidden treasures found in their street(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Strategic game show hosted by Dan Walker in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash(n)

programme.title: Coast and Country Auctions
programme.desc: A three-day sale at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, which sees horse enthusiasts gathering to buy and sell Welsh cobs, one of Britain's most popular breeds(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Paul Hollywood delves into his family history, finding out about his maternal grandfather's experiences in the Second World War, serving as an anti-aircraft gunner in Tunisia and Italy. The Great British Bake Off judge then digs deeper, learning about his Scottish ancestry - and how one of his forebears held down a very demanding job(n)

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Aiden Byrne puts three home cooks through a series of culinary challenges, one of which is to fillet a piece of fish perfectly. Sheree Murphy hosts(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Raj Bisram are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Christina values a pair of Wedgwood plates, while a gold guinea impresses Raj(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown, the Rev Kate Bottley, Joel Dommett and John Thomson test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by the latest couple to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off. Plus, in-depth analysis of the weekend's performances(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steps star Ian 'H' Watkins and actress and singer Claire Sweeney join experts James Braxton and David Harper on a quest to find items to sell at auction, with the friends scouring the Welsh Border for antiques. While H and James gamble a vast portion of their cash on two large items, Claire and David opt for toys and Victorian belt buckles(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts as first-round losers the Eggchasers and the Outliers return for one last chance to stay in the competition and reach the second stage. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Not specific, Of the body, Not public, and Not minor(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz begins, with two teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Vienna Blood
programme.desc: In 1900s Vienna, young English doctor Max Liebermann - studying under the famed psychoanalyst Sigmund Freud - comes into contact with Oskar Rheinhardt, a detective inspector struggling with the strange case of murder. Max offers his assistance, hoping his skills of perception and forensics and his deep understanding of human behaviour and deviance will be of use. Crime drama, starring Matthew Beard and Juergen Maurer(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Bollywood: The World's Biggest Film Industry
programme.desc: Part one of two. Anita Rani explores the world of Indian cinema, revealing the extraordinary stories and secrets behind an industry that employs more than a quarter of a million people. She begins her journey in Mumbai - the home of Bollywood - where the stars tower over the city on enormous murals. Anita discovers just how important the movies are to everyday people at one of the city's oldest cinemas, where she quickly finds herself in the middle of a screaming mob as the cast of that night's film make a surprise appearance(n)

programme.title: James May's Big Trouble in Model Britain
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary following a year at British toymaker Hornby Hobbies as it teeters on the brink of collapse. Director Simon Kohler assesses the state of his beloved business after his four-year absence, while master modeller Jim is given the biggest build of his career(n)

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: The first of three programmes in which cameras explore some of the most inhospitable places on the planet and the people who live there, including a team of Sherpa roping a route on Mount Everest's notorious Khumbu Icefall and, on the slopes of Mount Kilimanjaro, farmers fight pitched battles to save their crops from marauding elephants. In the Amazon rainforest, two boys undergo a rite of passage involving hundreds of ants with the most painful stings on Earth, and conservationists in the Grand Canyon try to ensure the survival of one of America's few surviving condor chicks(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Laure and Gilou are taken into custody, and Tintin is torn between his loyalties to his former colleagues and his new job. Josephine also faces a moral dilemma about how best to fight Lola's case. In French(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Cann plans to make a run for it as police close in, but first asks Edelman for help. Meanwhile, Laure and Gilou still have not been cleared of wrongdoing by Internal Affairs. In French(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Steve Wright and Caron Keating present the edition first aired on October 20, featuring performances by D Mob featuring Gary Haisman, the Christians, Enya, Deacon Blue, Milli Vanilli, Erasure, Kylie Minogue, Whitney Houston and The Wee Papa Girl Rappers(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Anthea Turner present the edition first aired on October 27, featuring performances by Milli Vanilli, the Art of Noise and Tom Jones, Royal House, Tanita Tikaram, Robert Palmer, Yazz, Deacon Blue, Enya, and the Beatmasters with PP Arnold(n)

programme.title: The Proclaimers: This Is the Story
programme.desc: Superfan David Tennant meets Craig and Charlie Reid of Scottish band the Proclaimers, as they discuss the 30 years since their debut album, and a career that has taken them from playing in small pubs and clubs around Scotland to become one of the nation's most iconic bands(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The war with the Lees escalates when they ransack the Shelbys' betting shop, while John reveals he intends to remarry, but his choice of bride does not go down well with his family. Chief Inspector Campbell puts more pressure on Thomas to deliver Freddie Thorne and the crate of stolen guns to him, while Grace begins to glean more information about the Peaky Blinders' illegal operations(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Thomas deals with an IRA chief who has come to Small Heath to avenge his cousin's death. Meanwhile, Campbell gets closer to finding the stolen guns and Grace has to decide whether her loyalties lie with the inspector or with Thomas. Period gangster drama, starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Thomas prepares to oust Billy Kimber at Worcester Racecourse, but secrets are revealed that force the Shelbys to face up to the problems that have divided them. Meanwhile, Campbell unleashes one last plan to destroy the Peaky Blinders and Grace finds herself with an impossible decision to make. Drama, starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield and Tottenham Hotspur v Arsenal at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium(n)

programme.title: The River Taff with Will Millard
programme.desc: Writer and fisherman Will Millard tells the story of how the once-polluted waterway was brought back from the dead to become one of the best salmon and trout rivers in Wales. Will travels from the source high in the Brecon Beacons National Park to Merthyr Tydfil, where he discovers that the town's ironworks were once vital to the British Empire. Along the way he trains with a mountain rescue team facing a unique set of challenges, meets a group of busy conservationists and fishes for wild brown trout in the most unlikely location(n)

programme.title: America's Greatest Opera House: The Story of the Met
programme.desc: The creation of the Metropolitan Opera's home of the last 50 years, focusing on the artists, architects and politicians who shaped the cultural life of New York in the '50s and 60s. Among the notable figures in the film are famed soprano Leontyne Price, who opened the new Met in 1966 in Samuel Barber's Antony and Cleopatra, and Rudolf Bing, the Met's general manager, who engineered the move from the old house to the new one(n)

programme.title: The Worlds of Ursula K Le Guin
programme.desc: An intimate insight into the real and imaginary worlds of Ursula K Le Guin, the author who helped to force the mainstream to embrace fantasy in literature. Produced with Le Guin's participation over the course of a decade, and featuring animation and reflections by other literary luminaries, the documentary is a journey of self-discovery through the writer's career(n)

programme.title: The Byrd Who Flew Alone: The Triumphs & Tragedy of Gene Clark
programme.desc: A documentary on the enigmatic former Byrds frontman Gene Clark, drawing on interviews with his family, friends and fellow musicians. Since the Missouri-born songwriter's death in 1991 at the age of 46, his songs have been covered by artists ranging from Robert Plant to Yo La Tengo, and he has been hailed as a key influence by generations of musicians - and yet arguably failed to enjoy the success his work deserved(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: As he finally begins to piece the puzzle together, Ambrose pursues a suspect. Meanwhile, Cora is paid a shock visit. Series finale. Violence/sex/mature themes(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Thomas is determined to expand the legal and illegal sides of the Shelby family business by taking on various interests in the south of England, but an enemy from his past looks set to stand in his way. Cillian Murphy stars(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Enigmatic mobster Alfie Solomons crosses paths with Tommy while the latter is in London searching for Polly's long-lost children. Solomons is a dangerous man, and Tommy will be lucky to get away unscathed. Meanwhile, ghosts from Arthur's military past continue to haunt his troubled mind(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Clarissa and the King's Cookbook
programme.desc: Clarissa Dickson Wright tracks down The Forme of Cury, a 700-year-old scroll written from recipes created by King Richard II's master chefs that is Britain's oldest known cookbook. The presenter uses traditional methods to prepare dishes ranging from venison and beaver to pike and lampreys(n)

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary profile of the Queen's sister, whose life and loves reflected the social and sexual revolution that transformed Britain during the 20th century. With archive footage and interviews, the series follows Margaret's life as she redefined the image of the modern princess, and begins with her birth in 1930. The abdication of her uncle propelled Margaret to second in line to the throne, but her life would be shaped by dramatic social change as Britain entered a more egalitarian era after the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The continuing impact of China's one-child policy, which was abandoned in 2015 but has left the country with an ageing population and tens of millions more men than women. With new information on tens of thousands of abandoned and kidnapped children, nearly all of them infant girls, the film breaks open decades of silence on a vast, unprecedented social experiment that shaped - and destroyed - countless lives(n)

programme.title: The Sun
programme.desc: Documentary examining the human race's relationship with Earth's nearest star. The programme examines how Stone Age monuments were aligned with its movements across the sky, while modern-day scientists reveal how they have found new ways to observe the sun, and hope to be able to harness its power. Narrated by Andrew Lincoln(n)

programme.title: Into the Wind

Maddie follows the journey that wool takes, from shearing a sheep to going through the factory, to end up in the carpets we have at home. Nez calls in outside help to check the security of the agency's website and Elaine samples the very latest Welsh fusion cuisine at a pop-up dining experience. Guest starring Nick Helm and Cariad Lloyd(n)

programme.title: Beauty Queen and Single
programme.desc: Working mum and Miss European runner up 2008 Sinita braves dating minus the make-up(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan comperes an evening of stand-up at London's Hammersmith Apollo, treating the audience to his own brand of humour before introducing routines by comedy circuit stars Jason Byrne and Stewart Francis(n)

programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Cleveland Browns v Pittsburgh Steelers at FirstEnergy Stadium(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Business owners Paul and Tracy Nicholls and their children head out for a trial week living in New Zealand, to see if the move abroad would offer them a more relaxed pace of life(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Kate Bliss assist two teams at an antiques fair in Oswestry, Shropshire, while presenter Eric Knowles visits a Cold War bunker in rural Cheshire(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown hosts the first of two programmes to find the Gospel Choir of the Year, with five amateur groups from across the country taking to the stage at Reading Town Hall. They have to impress three new judges in the form of vocal coach Mark De-Lisser, West End star Brenda Edwards and pop singer Rick Astley(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: An appeal for funds on behalf of Sense(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle return to Longleat Safari Park, where Jean Johansson helps new penguins to settle in and cameras try to capture the birth of a cheetah cub(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: As more people worry about the use of single-use plastic, cameras follow the Sainsbury's innovation team as they try to find a recyclable alternative for the plastic bag used to carry loose fruit and vegetables. But as it is launched across the country, are customers ready for such a change in shopping habits? With Easter around the corner, the supply staff decide what to stock in each store and how much, while at the clothing HQ, plans are already being drawn up for this year's Christmas fashion(n)

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sophie Evans find out how people across Wales have been raising money for this year's appeal, and see how the money donated benefits young people in Wales(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team heads to the Balcaskie Estate in the East Neuk of Fife, where Charlotte Smith tries her hand at competitive ploughing, Ellie Harrison meets some local food producers, and Steve Brown is at Pittenweem harbour as the fishing boats bring in their catches. Meanwhile, it's a tense time for Adam Henson down on the farm as his cattle are tested for TB(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs and Isolated for millions of years, leaving weird and wonderful animals found nowhere else on Earth. In its jungles, a cassowary - one of the most dangerous birds in the world - stands six feet tall, while kangaroos and wombats brave snowstorms, and gum-tree forests are filled with predators. In its red desert heart, reptiles drink through their skin and huge flocks of wild budgerigars swirl in search of water. On secret islands Tasmanian devils roam, and offshore thousands of sharks gather for a rare event.(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman host from Blackpool's Tower Ballroom as they announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores taking part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. With music by Westlife(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: After escaping from the clutches of the Gobblers, Lyra finds help from an unlikely source. With their assistance she is able to piece together more about her past and keep herself safe from Mrs Coulter and the Magisterium. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen and Ruth Wilson(n)

programme.title: The War of the Worlds
programme.desc: When a mysterious capsule lands in Woking, Surrey, in 1905 lovers George and Amy join the curious crowd to examine it. As events unfold, the couple are caught in the middle of a Martian invasion of Earth, fighting for their love and their lives against an enemy beyond their comprehension. Adaptation of HG Wells' sci-fi classic, starring Rafe Spall, Eleanor Tomlinson, Rupert Graves and Robert Carlyle(n)

programme.title: The Richard Dimbleby Lecture
programme.desc: Jonathan Dimbleby introduces this year's lecture from London's Design Museum, with engineer and computer scientist Tim Berners-Lee talking about his aspirations for the future of the world wide web, which he invented in 1989(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield and Tottenham Hotspur v Arsenal at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium(n)

programme.title: Gary Lineker: My Grandad's War
programme.desc: The Match of the Day presenter retraces the footsteps of his grandfather Stanley Abbs to explore a brutal but often overlooked chapter of the Second World War. Using the official war diary kept by Stanley's unit as a guide, Gary revisits the places where he was stationed and meets experts who help him piece together what happened to his grandfather during the Italian campaign. Along the way, Gary meets veterans who share first-hand testimonies to and help him understand the reality of combat in Italy(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Everyone is reeling from the events of last week but the big question is - has Sharon managed to keep her secret hidden? Ben realises Callum is seriously considering joining the police and admits that would be the end of their relationship. Mick encourages Linda to drum up business in his bid to win a best pub competition(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Rachel Treadaway-Williams investigates a builder whose shoddy work shocked trading standards officers. Plus, a report from a specialist unit to find out how fitness trackers monitor sleep(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Government announced the closure of investigations into alleged war crimes in Iraq and Afghanistan before a single soldier was prosecuted. But has there been a cover-up at the highest levels of the British military? Reporter Richard Bilton meets UK detectives who talk for the first time about how they were prevented from prosecuting soldiers suspected of serious crimes, revealing evidence that suggests the Ministry of Defence and senior officers were involved in the cover-up of torture and illegal killings(n)

programme.title: Tourist Trap
programme.desc: Elaine attends a business networking event for powerful women and bumps into old friend Meinir, owner of the Organic Wellness Centre in Swansea. Meanwhile, the team promotes healthy living, with Wyn making the case for cycling in Wales as he dons lycra to showcase his pedalling prowess(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor is jealous when new pupil Gavin - who has childhood ties with Jock - arrives at school, and he is determined not to be ousted as best friend. Conor struggles to stand up to Gavin - but it's not easy as the newcomer's mother has just started teaching at the school. Meanwhile, a spate of thefts leads Principal Walsh to patrolling the school grounds to monitor any potential stealing(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space tell stories of life on our planet from a brand new perspective, to reveal new discoveries and explore Earth's most remote habitats. In the third episode, cameras unveil stories behind some of the planet's most intriguing colours, including the lights of the aurora, which tower in vertical streaks hundreds of kilometres high. Elsewhere, cameras also capture vast phytoplankton blooms in the ocean, and millions of rapeseed flowers in bloom in China's Yunnan province(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Nina Warhurst present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Halesowen in Birmingham and an end terrace in Northwood in Stoke on Trent are sold under the hammer(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Comedian Joe Pasquale and ventriloquist Paul Zerdin take on Debbie McGee and Sooty puppeteer Richard Cadell in a bid to raise money for Children in Need. Experts Tim Weeks and Thomas Forrester assist them in their endeavours, while Natasha Raskin Sharp presents(n)

programme.title: Live International Rugby Union
programme.desc: Barbarians v Fiji (Kick-off 2.30pm). John Inverdale presents coverage of the fixture from Twickenham, as the invitational side face the Pacific Islanders. The Barbarians' unique blend of players from around the world are often easy on the eye, while Fiji - fresh from giving Wales a scare at the World Cup in October - will be keen to make an impact. With studio analysis from Martin Johnson and Ben Ryan, and commentary by Eddie Butler and Brian Moore(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers help Naomi and Chris in Burton on Trent, who struggle with their steeply sloping plot and love their motorbikes more than their garden(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special family edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Ade Edmondson, Beattie Edmondson, Helen Richardson-Walsh, Kate Richardson-Walsh, Dr Ranj Singh, Min Singh, Mark Steel and Elliot Steel(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the return of the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In the first episode, sisters Sara and Vanessa from London, friends John and Rachel from Glasgow, and father and son duo Fluff and Freddie from Kent take part(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the celebrity couples take to the dancefloor at Blackpool's Tower Ballroom - minus Mike Bushell and Katya Jones, who were eliminated last weekend. Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of 10 before the voting lines open - giving viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the final episode of the series, Carol and Aimee, a mother and daughter from Bristol, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A routine CQC investigation has huge ramifications for both the staff and patients at the hospital. Meanwhile, Robyn is devastated when she realises she cannot rely on Duffy, Ethan stifles his feelings and turns his back on Effie, and Rash protects Mason from Dylan. Starring Amanda Henderson, Cathy Shipton and Derek Thompson(n)

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: Acting Sergeant Ken Beattie gets a shock as he solves a murderous cold case, PC Jack McLaren meets his match and Chief Commissioner Miekelson has another bright idea. Meanwhile, Sir Tom Hunter cannot resist the opportunity to invest on the latest business idea from Chief Commissioner Cameron Miekelson(n)

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Cyprus v Scotland. Jonathan Sutherland presents action the Euro 2020 Group I qualifier at GSP Stadium. Scotland are looking to complete a double over the Cypriots, having won 2-1 when the sides met at Hampden Park in June(n)

programme.title: Guardians of the Galaxy (2014)
programme.desc: A boy abducted by aliens grows up to be an interstellar mercenary. He and a band of misfits break out of a prison space station with a plan to sell a mysterious orb to the highest bidder - but cross a warlord, who intends to turn it into a powerful weapon. Sci-fi adventure, starring Chris Pratt, Zoe Saldana and Dave Bautista, with the voices of Bradley Cooper and Vin Diesel(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in South America, where animals must specialise if they are to carve out a niche. In Patagonia, a puma mother draws on a lifetime's experience to catch prey three times her weight, while rarely seen Andean bears clamber 30 metres into the cloud forest canopy to find elusive fruit. Poison dart frogs use ingenious methods to keep their tadpoles safe, anacondas stalk capuchin monkeys, and swifts make death-defying flights through Igauzu Falls on the border between Argentina and Brazil(n)

programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the final episode of the series, Carol and Aimee, a mother and daughter from Bristol, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A routine CQC investigation has huge ramifications for both the staff and patients at the hospital. Meanwhile, Robyn is devastated when she realises she cannot rely on Duffy, Ethan stifles his feelings and turns his back on Effie, and Rash protects Mason from Dylan. Starring Amanda Henderson, Cathy Shipton and Derek Thompson(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Northern Ireland v Netherlands. Gavin Andrews presents action from the Euro 2020 Group C qualifier from Windsor Park. Michael O'Neill's men gave the Dutch a scare in last month's reverse fixture in Rotterdam when substitute Josh Magennis gave them a 1-0 lead, but Holland fought back with two late goals by Memphis Depay and one from Luuk de Jong. With analysis by Stephen Craigan and commentary from Thomas Kane(n)

programme.title: Guardians of the Galaxy (2014)
programme.desc: A boy abducted by aliens grows up to be an interstellar mercenary. He and a band of misfits break out of a prison space station with a plan to sell a mysterious orb to the highest bidder - but cross a warlord, who intends to turn it into a powerful weapon. Sci-fi adventure, starring Chris Pratt, Zoe Saldana and Dave Bautista, with the voices of Bradley Cooper and Vin Diesel(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Cleveland Browns v Pittsburgh Steelers at FirstEnergy Stadium(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around the Province, with Mark Carruthers(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Ria Hebden present topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Business owners Paul and Tracy Nicholls and their children head out for a trial week living in New Zealand, to see if the move abroad would offer them a more relaxed pace of life(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Kate Bliss assist two teams at an antiques fair in Oswestry, Shropshire, while presenter Eric Knowles visits a Cold War bunker in rural Cheshire(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown hosts the first of two programmes to find the Gospel Choir of the Year, with five amateur groups from across the country taking to the stage at Reading Town Hall. They have to impress three new judges in the form of vocal coach Mark De-Lisser, West End star Brenda Edwards and pop singer Rick Astley(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: An appeal for funds on behalf of Sense(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble celebrate the life of silverback gorilla Nico, which died at the age of 56, and pay tribute to the powerful bonds formed between keepers and animals(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers help Naomi and Chris in Burton on Trent, who struggle with their steeply sloping plot and love their motorbikes more than their garden(n)

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Holly Hamilton and Stephen Clements present hightlights of the fundraising extravaganza and look at how it supports children and young people here in Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team heads to the Balcaskie Estate in the East Neuk of Fife, where Charlotte Smith tries her hand at competitive ploughing, Ellie Harrison meets some local food producers, and Steve Brown is at Pittenweem harbour as the fishing boats bring in their catches. Meanwhile, it's a tense time for Adam Henson down on the farm as his cattle are tested for TB(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs and Isolated for millions of years, leaving weird and wonderful animals found nowhere else on Earth. In its jungles, a cassowary - one of the most dangerous birds in the world - stands six feet tall, while kangaroos and wombats brave snowstorms, and gum-tree forests are filled with predators. In its red desert heart, reptiles drink through their skin and huge flocks of wild budgerigars swirl in search of water. On secret islands Tasmanian devils roam, and offshore thousands of sharks gather for a rare event.(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman host from Blackpool's Tower Ballroom as they announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores taking part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. With music by Westlife(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: After escaping from the clutches of the Gobblers, Lyra finds help from an unlikely source. With their assistance she is able to piece together more about her past and keep herself safe from Mrs Coulter and the Magisterium. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen and Ruth Wilson(n)

programme.title: The War of the Worlds
programme.desc: When a mysterious capsule lands in Woking, Surrey, in 1905 lovers George and Amy join the curious crowd to examine it. As events unfold, the couple are caught in the middle of a Martian invasion of Earth, fighting for their love and their lives against an enemy beyond their comprehension. Adaptation of HG Wells' sci-fi classic, starring Rafe Spall, Eleanor Tomlinson, Rupert Graves and Robert Carlyle(n)

programme.title: The Richard Dimbleby Lecture
programme.desc: Jonathan Dimbleby introduces this year's lecture from London's Design Museum, with engineer and computer scientist Tim Berners-Lee talking about his aspirations for the future of the world wide web, which he invented in 1989(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield and Tottenham Hotspur v Arsenal at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium(n)

programme.title: Gary Lineker: My Grandad's War
programme.desc: The Match of the Day presenter retraces the footsteps of his grandfather Stanley Abbs to explore a brutal but often overlooked chapter of the Second World War. Using the official war diary kept by Stanley's unit as a guide, Gary revisits the places where he was stationed and meets experts who help him piece together what happened to his grandfather during the Italian campaign. Along the way, Gary meets veterans who share first-hand testimonies to and help him understand the reality of combat in Italy(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood spends 24 hours working as a firefighter in Texas, but just getting the gear on in time is a tough job for him, let alone confronting his fear of heights(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell views a Macclesfield house with worrying cracks, Dion Dublin finds a semi in Cumbria that offers potential and Martin Roberts is puzzled by an unusual lot in Kent(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A man uses a burglary as an opportunity to exaggerate his claim, while friendships are tested as hard-up students resort to fraudulent measures. Naga Munchetty presents(n)

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: City of London police go on the trail of an international fraudster, while one of Britain's largest-ever criminal enterprises is taken down by Border Force and the Royal Navy(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items at the Royal Cornwall Showground, while presenter Charlie Ross visits Smeaton's Tower in Plymouth(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel and Zara conduct interviews for a new practice manager, while Karen finds herself more deeply involved in Martha's personal life and Al encounters an odd family set-up(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth struggles to protect those closest to him when an old foe is unexpectedly released from prison(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a house-hunting mission in the Northumberland countryside with a couple who have their heart set on a character stone property with holiday let potential(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne visits Woking Recycling Centre, on a mission to save three tip-bound items. He enlists the help of Sarah Peterson to give an outdated telephone cabinet a radical revamp(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which experts try to restore family heirlooms and treasured possessions to their former glories. In the first edition, Jay Blades and the team take on an ornate walnut piano stool, a speedometer from a 1920s vintage car and baby's wicker crib(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Social Democratic and Labour Party(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Everyone is reeling from the events of last week but the big question is - has Sharon managed to keep her secret hidden? Ben realises Callum is seriously considering joining the police and admits that would be the end of their relationship. Mick encourages Linda to drum up business in his bid to win a best pub competition(n)

programme.title: Barra on the Bann
programme.desc: Barra Best continues his 100-mile journey from the point where the Upper Bann flows into Lough Neagh. He meets a group of hardy open-water swimmers, tries his hand at water-skiing, and takes his father on a fishing trip with some of Europe's finest anglers(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Government announced the closure of investigations into alleged war crimes in Iraq and Afghanistan before a single soldier was prosecuted. But has there been a cover-up at the highest levels of the British military? Reporter Richard Bilton meets UK detectives who talk for the first time about how they were prevented from prosecuting soldiers suspected of serious crimes, revealing evidence that suggests the Ministry of Defence and senior officers were involved in the cover-up of torture and illegal killings(n)

programme.title: On the Frontline
programme.desc: Until Northern Ireland spiralled into mayhem after the explosive summer of 1969, Northern Ireland's firefighters did the same job as any other fire service - days of relative inactivity interspersed with house fires, road crashes and the occasional factory fire. During the Troubles they responded to many thousands of incidents, which brought death and misery on a horrendous scale. Here some of those firefighters share their experiences(n)

programme.title: Pop Goes Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A look back at the events of 1996, the year the peace process was stalled over the issue of decommissioning and the IRA withdrew their ceasefire with bombings in Canary Wharf and Manchester city centre. In July, the Orange Order is granted permission to march down the Garvaghy Road from Drumcree Church, a demonstration met with rioting in nationalist areas(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor is jealous when new pupil Gavin - who has childhood ties with Jock - arrives at school, and he is determined not to be ousted as best friend. Conor struggles to stand up to Gavin - but it's not easy as the newcomer's mother has just started teaching at the school. Meanwhile, a spate of thefts leads Principal Walsh to patrolling the school grounds to monitor any potential stealing(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

Simon Cowell mentors the Groups, Nicole Scherzinger is in charge of the Overs, and Louis Walsh handles the Unders, and as usual the judges are each allowed to send one act through to next week by pushing the golden buzzer, leaving the rest at the mercy of the voting public(n)

There is a surprise for Dec as he finds out he has close family he never knew existed, while Ant's history takes him to the United Nations. After a whirlwind tour of the US, it's back to Ireland to investigate a family secret that Ant's family has been trying to unlock for years(n)

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: Kosovo v England (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of both teams' concluding Group A fixture, held at Fadil Vokrri Stadium in Pristina. September's reverse fixture saw England claim a 5-3 victory in Southampton, but Kosovo will have taken heart from their second-half display after being 5-1 down at the break. Gareth Southgate will hope his side defend better on this occasion while matching the attacking vigour that was shown in the first half. With analysis from Lee Dixon, Roy Keane and Ashley Cole, and commentary by Clive Tyldesley and Glenn Hoddle(n)

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!

As usual, it's an eclectic line-up of star names, drawn from the worlds of TV, sport and entertainment, featuring former footballer Ian Wright, Girls Aloud singer Nadine Coyle, Radio 1 presenter Adele Roberts, comedian Andrew Maxwell, former EastEnders actress Jacqueline Jossa, DJ Roman Kemp, Olympian and TV personality Caitlin Jenner, ex-England rugby player James Haskell, RakSu singer Myles Stephenson, and TV and radio presenter Kate Garraway(n)

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: Kosovo v England. Mark Pougatch presents action from both teams' concluding Group A fixture, held at Fadil Vokrri Stadium in Pristina. programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha joins forces with Mandy Yin, the guru of Malaysian cuisine, to create a family feast from Malaysia. The menu comprises Kaya French toast for breakfast, king prawn laksa for lunch, Nadia's chicken curry for dinner and Mandy's ble pandan pancakes for dessert(n)

programme.title: Riding a Dream
programme.desc: The remarkable story of 18-year-old British Muslim woman Khadijah Mellah, and her journey to the 2019 Magnolia Cup. From her inner city riding school to one of the most prestigious horse racing events in the world, this film follows her ups and downs as she gets ready for the race of her life(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 2.25 Betvictor Gold Cup Handicap Steeple Chase from Cheltenham, and races at 1.50, 3.00 and 3.30. Plus, races from 2.45 and 3.15 from Lingfield Park. With analysis from AP McCoy and Ruby Walsh, reports by Matt Chapman, Alice Plunkett, Luke Harvey, Oli Bell and Mick Fitzgerald, and commentary from Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets a nine-month-old terrier who was found wandering the streets whilst heavily pregnant, before spending time with a three-month-old puppy called Pancake, who is having problems with his eyesight. Elsewhere, a dog arrives at Battersea who was found dumped with a terrible case of mange and an injured leg(n)

programme.title: The Mountain
programme.desc: A look at the busy summer season, when it is all-hands-on-deck as the epic Snowdon Race brings runners from all over the world to the mountain. Farmers Jo and Fiona deal with the frustrations of the season's busy months as unruly pets attack their livestock, while big crowds bring problems for Rob and the mountain rescue team.(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Coronation Street's Hayley Tamaddon, football star Geoff Hurst, comedian Rob Beckett and presenter Gaby Roslin pit their wits against ruthless quiz geniuses in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: The latest group of men entering the 'love lift' are a tech-savvy personal trainer, an airline pilot, a magician and musician, and a city banker, all hoping to impress a studio full of single women and win a date - but must first get the 30 female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Paddy McGuinness presents(n)

programme.title: The X Factor: Celebrity
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts the star-studded version of the singing contest, in which the line-up of famous faces leave their comfort zones and take to the stage to perform - and while some have proved to be naturals, others have struggled somewhat. Simon Cowell mentors the Groups, Nicole Scherzinger is in charge of the Overs, and Louis Walsh handles the Unders, and as usual the judges are each allowed to send one act through to next week by pushing the golden buzzer, leaving the rest at the mercy of the voting public(n)

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by another group of celebrity guests, including comedian John Bishop, who is currently on a UK tour, The Great British Bake Off's Paul Hollywood, rising stand-up Suzi Ruffell, and Naomi Ackie, soon to be seen in the next Star Wars movie. Plus, Halsey performs in the studio(n)

programme.title: Ant & Dec's DNA Journey
programme.desc: Part two of two. Anthony McPartlin and Declan Donnelly embark on a journey to New York to follow the bloodlines of their mothers using DNA analysis and genealogy. There is a surprise for Dec as he finds out he has close family he never knew existed, while Ant's history takes him to the United Nations. After a whirlwind tour of the US, it's back to Ireland to investigate a family secret that Ant's family has been trying to unlock for years(n)

programme.title: Bang on the Money

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: Kosovo v England (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of both teams' concluding Group A fixture, held at Fadil Vokrri Stadium in Pristina. September's reverse fixture saw England claim a 5-3 victory in Southampton, but Kosovo will have taken heart from their second-half display after being 5-1 down at the break. Gareth Southgate will hope his side defend better on this occasion while matching the attacking vigour that was shown in the first half. With analysis from Lee Dixon, Roy Keane and Ashley Cole, and commentary by Clive Tyldesley and Glenn Hoddle(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: To mark the start of a new series of I'm a Celebrity - Get Me Out of Here, Bradley Walsh presents as 2018 campmates Rita Simons, Malique Thompson-Dwyer, Fleur East and James McVey try to win as much money as they dare before teaming up in the final round against one of the show's five quiz experts. But which Chaser will the campmates be facing tonight?(n)

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: Ant and Dec present as a fresh batch of famous faces wave goodbye to their lives of luxury and set up home among the creepy-crawlies of the Australian jungle for up to three weeks. As usual, it's an eclectic line-up of star names, drawn from the worlds of TV, sport and entertainment, featuring former footballer Ian Wright, Girls Aloud singer Nadine Coyle, Radio 1 presenter Adele Roberts, comedian Andrew Maxwell, former EastEnders actress Jacqueline Jossa, DJ Roman Kemp, Olympian and TV personality Caitlin Jenner, ex-England rugby player James Haskell, RakSu singer Myles Stephenson, and TV and radio presenter Kate Garraway(n)

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: Kosovo v England. Mark Pougatch presents action from both teams' concluding Group A fixture, held at Fadil Vokrri Stadium in Pristina. Plus, the goals from Luxembourg v Portugal and Albania v France. With analysis from Lee Dixon and Glenn Hoddle(n)

programme.title: England's Grand
